question: [File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TracingMiddleware does not inspect or validate the SignedTransaction field of a /construction/submit request body, allowing them to submit a transaction where the signature is valid but the transaction body has been tampered with (e.g., modified recipient), and the middleware logs only HTTP 200 without any indication of the tampered content?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/submit request with a very long HTTP method string (e.g., 10,000 characters), causing TracingMiddleware's fmt.Sprintf to allocate a large string per request and, at scale, exhaust server memory, preventing legitimate transaction submissions from being processed?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that TracingMiddleware logs time.Since(start) after inner.ServeHTTP completes, meaning the logged duration includes the full transaction submission time, to use timing side-channels to infer whether a tampered transaction was accepted or rejected by the network before the HTTP response is returned?

[File: 'File Name: rosetta/app/middleware/trace.go -> Scope: High: Any impacts caused by Tampering with submitted transactions'] [Function: TracingMiddleware()] Can an unprivileged external user send a /construction/submit request with a path that matches a regex pattern used by a WAF or IDS to identify internal health check paths (e.g., '/health/liveness%2F../construction/submit'), causing the WAF to whitelist the request while TracingMiddleware routes it to the construction submit handler with a tampered transaction?
